articles.title: Colombia’s Urban Unemployment Rate Falls to 9.2% in February 2026
articles.expand: Colombia’s urban unemployment rate eased to 9.2% in February 2026, down from 10.6% recorded in January 2026, according to the latest data updated on 30 March 2026. The improvement signals a notable month-on-month recovery in urban labor market conditions. The 1.4 percentage point decline suggests that hiring activity in Colombia’s cities strengthen...

articles.title: Euro area: Inflation jump and hawkish ECB stance – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale economists expect Euro area headline inflation to rise sharply in March, driven by higher energy costs, while core inflation eases slightly.

articles.title: Powell speech: Policy in good place to wait and see
articles.expand: Federal Reserve (Fed) Chairman Jerome Powell said that there is tension between the Fed's two objectives, while participating in a moderated discussion at the Harvard University Principles of Economics Class in Cambridge, per Reuters.

articles.title: Oil: Escalation risks from Iran conflict – NBC
articles.expand: National Bank of Canada’s (NBC) Angelo Katsoras outlines how an Iran conflict could severely disrupt Oil and gas markets if key energy infrastructure and the Strait of Hormuz are targeted.

articles.title: Gold holds firm as yields ease, US Dollar strength caps gains
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) struggles to gain traction on Monday, trimming part of its earlier gains as escalating tensions in the Middle East and shifting interest rate expectations keep markets volatile.

articles.title: USD: Gradual depreciation outlook – BNP Paribas
articles.expand: BNP Paribas analysts expect the US economy to grow above potential in 2026, with GDP at 2.7% and inflation at 3.1%, keeping the Federal Reserve on hold with the Fed Funds target range at 3.5%-3.75%.

articles.title: BoE: On hold as CPI stays above target – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale economists note the United Kingdom (UK) markets still price around 70 bps of Bank of England (BoE) easing in 2026 despite Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) pushback, while the bank’s team expects no cuts this year.

articles.title: BoC: Dovish tone and long hold signalled – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities analysts anticipate the Bank of Canada’s (BoC) March Summary of Deliberations will maintain a dovish tone, stressing excess supply and disinflation.

articles.title: AUD/USD weakens on Middle East tensions as markets await RBA minutes
articles.expand: AUD/USD trades around 0.6860 on Monday at the time of writing, down 0.21% on the day, as investors adopt a cautious stance amid escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

articles.title: Germany: War-driven energy shock risks ECB hike – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Senior Economist Dr. Ralph Solveen notes that Germany’s inflation rate rose from 1.9% to 2.7% in March 2026 as energy prices surged due to the war in Iran, while core inflation stayed at 2.5%.

articles.expand: Rabobank’s Senior FX Strategist Jane Foley notes that the Pound has been the second best performing G10 currency after the Dollar since the Middle East conflict, driven by a sharp repricing of Bank of England (BoE) policy expectations.

articles.expand: ING’s Chief Economist Bert Colijn highlights that Eurozone sentiment weakened in March as the Middle East war hit confidence. The economic sentiment indicator dropped from 98.3 in February to 96.6, with businesses and consumers more worried about inflation.

articles.title: USD/CAD extends six-day advance as geopolitical tensions bolster US Dollar
articles.expand: USD/CAD trades around 1.3910 on Monday at the time of writing, up 0.12% on the day and extending its winning streak to six consecutive days. The pair is supported by a stronger US Dollar (USD) amid rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

articles.title: Euro area economic sentiment eases in March as Middle East conflict stokes inflation fears
articles.expand: Euro area economic confidence slipped to 96.6 in March, after recording a revised 98.2 reading in February. That's the lowest estimate since September with consumer confidence (-16.3) also falling back on the month to its lowest since the latter months of 2023. It's all coming undone as the US-Iran war stirs up inflation fears in Europe once again....

articles.title: Swiss KOF Barometer Drops Sharply to 96.1, Signals Broad-Based Economic Weakness
articles.expand: Switzerland’s KOF Economic Barometer fell sharply from 103.8 to 96.1 in March, missing expectations of 100.6 and pointing to a notable deterioration in growth momentum. The decline reflects a broad-based slowdown, with both production and demand-side indicators weakening, suggesting that the economy is losing traction more quickly than anticipated....
